content: Micah 7:18 — 'Who is a God like You, ___ iniquity?'
explanation: The LORD pardons iniquity and does not retain His anger forever, because He delights in steadfast love.

content: Micah 4:3 prophesies an age of peace: 'They shall beat their swords into ___'
explanation: Weapons turn into farming tools. They shall not learn war anymore.

content: Micah declares that he is filled with power because of what?
explanation: Micah 3:8 — Power for ministry comes from the Spirit of the LORD, not from the prophet himself.
options: The Spirit of the LORD, with justice and might

content: The Messiah from Bethlehem will shepherd His people in what?
explanation: Micah 5:4 — The great Shepherd shall stand and shepherd His flock in the strength of the LORD.

content: Micah 6:6-7 asks: Does the LORD want thousands of rams or rivers of oil?
explanation: God does not want religious form but a life of justice and humility.
options: No — God wants justice, kindness, and humility

content: Against which cities does Micah warn that the LORD's judgment will come?
explanation: Micah 1:5 — Both capitals had sinned and both would be judged. God is just toward all.

content: Micah prophesies: in the latter days, what will become of the mountain of the LORD's house?
explanation: Micah 4:1-2 — God's kingdom will be exalted and all peoples will come to learn His ways.
options: Established as the highest of the mountains, and peoples shall flow to it

content: Micah declares: 'But as for me, I will look to ___'
explanation: Micah 7:7 — Whatever the circumstance, Micah chooses to wait for and trust the LORD.

content: Micah 7:8 — 'When I fall, I shall ___; when I sit in darkness, the LORD will be a light to me.'
explanation: Though we fall, the believer will rise again, for the LORD is light in darkness.

content: Micah 5:2 prophesies that the Messiah will be born in which city?
explanation: Micah 5:2 — 'But you, O Bethlehem Ephrathah... from you shall come forth for me one who is to be ruler in Israel.' Fulfilled in Matthew 2:1.

content: Micah 6:8 sums up what the LORD requires of man as what?
explanation: Micah 6:8 — Three things the LORD requires: do justice, love kindness, walk humbly with God. Simple yet profound.
options: Do justice, love kindness, and walk humbly with your God

content: From which town did the prophet Micah come?
explanation: Micah 1:1 — 'The word of the LORD that came to Micah of Moresheth in the days of Jotham, Ahaz, and Hezekiah, kings of Judah.'

content: What does Micah 2:1-2 condemn people for doing on their beds at night?
explanation: Micah 2:1 — 'Woe to those who devise wickedness and work evil on their beds! When the morning dawns, they perform it, because it is in the power of their hand.'

content: What does Micah 2:12-13 promise God will do with the remnant of Israel?
explanation: Micah 2:12 — 'I will surely assemble all of you, O Jacob; I will gather the remnant of Israel.'

content: According to Micah 3:11, what did Jerusalem's leaders and priests do?
explanation: Micah 3:11 — 'Its heads give judgment for a bribe; its priests teach for a price; its prophets practice divination for money.'
options: Judged for a bribe and taught for a price

content: Under what does Micah 4:4 say each person will sit in the time of peace?
explanation: Micah 4:4 — 'But they shall sit every man under his vine and under his fig tree, and no one shall make them afraid.'

content: Where does Micah 4:10 say Zion will go before being delivered?
explanation: Micah 4:10 — 'You shall go to Babylon; there you shall be rescued; there the LORD will redeem you from the hand of your enemies.'

content: What does Micah 5:5 say the One coming from Bethlehem will be for His people?
explanation: Micah 5:5 — 'And he shall be their peace. When the Assyrian comes into our land...'

content: According to Micah 6:14-15, what consequence comes upon the people for their sin?
explanation: Micah 6:14 — 'You shall eat, but not be satisfied, and there shall be hunger within you... You shall sow, but not reap.'
options: They will eat to the full
Eat but not be satisfied; sow but not reap

content: Where does Micah 7:19 say God will cast the people's sins?
explanation: Micah 7:19 — 'He will again have compassion on us; he will tread our iniquities underfoot. You will cast all our sins into the depths of the sea.'
options: Into the depths of the sea
